title: Instance members should not write to static fields
description: Instance members should not write to "static" fieldsAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode Smellmulti-threadingThis rule raises an issue each time astaticfield is updated from a non-static method or property.Why is this an issue?More InfoUpdating astaticfield from a non-staticmethod introduces significant challenges and potential bugs. Multiple class instances and threads can access and modify thestaticfield concurrently, leading to unintended consequences for other instances or threads (unexpected behavior,race conditionsand synchronization problems).class MyClass { private static int count = 0; public void DoSomething() { //... count++; // Noncompliant: make the enclosing instance property 'static' or remove this set on the 'static' field.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects non-static methods/properties writing to static fields
logic.triggering_conditions: (Instance method contains assignment to static field) OR (Instance property writes to static field)
exclusion_conditions: Static methods writing to static fields (allowed)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects race condition risks when instance methods modify static fields. Pattern matches: (1) non-static method bodies containing 'staticField = ' or 'staticField++', (2) assignments to fields declared with 'static' keyword. Excludes static methods (safe to modify static fields) and instance fields. AST-based approach examines MethodDeclaration nodes for static field modifications.

should_trigger: public void DoSomething() { count++; } // where count is static
public void UpdateShared(string value) { sharedData = value; } // where sharedData is static
should_not_trigger: public void DoSomething() { instanceCount++; } // instance field
public static void IncrementCount() { count++; } // static method
